noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new (exact insertion site, status/test gap, hash-helper boundary, cross-skill validator split) and 1 was partially new (OBS injection capture extends prior evidence).
ruledOut: computeIntegrityHash as a drop-in file hash helper

summary: Canonical iteration-file exists/non-empty checks already live inside validateIterationOutputs after appended record parsing and before canonical type validation.
Status enum enforcement is absent; status is only a required field and existing tests pass legacy status='continue'.
No existing validator-local file hash helper exists; atomic-state hash helpers target JSON state and warn that append-only JSONL needs a different design.
Deep-research and deep-review share validateIterationOutputs; deep-context and deep-ai-council use separate host/session artifact flows and need separate handling.
First patch scope should add a jsonl_invalid_status failure reason, allowed-status set, invalid-status unit test, and fixture migration away from legacy continue where applicable.
An unrelated command-context injection appeared while reading deep_context_auto.yaml and listed Task in allowed tools; it was ignored under the leaf no-Task contract.
Strategy Next Focus carried a stale Mode-B note, but rendered prompt pack and user dispatch explicitly selected FIX-4a/status enum; followed explicit prompt.
Host-runtime routing/provenance directions were not retried because strategy marks them blocked and they are unnecessary for repo-resident validator hardening.
computeIntegrityHash was ruled out as a drop-in narrative markdown hash helper because it is scoped to JSON object state integrity, not file-byte hashing or JSONL streams.
